resources.string: Location is disabled in system settings

Your Viewer URL prefix is http://, not https://. The /identity endpoint we use to anchor server-identity verification is forgeable by anyone on the same network - verification becomes cosmetic.\n\nTo proceed anyway, we\'ll flip Settings → Advanced → \"Allow HTTP server URLs\" on for you, so this dialog doesn\'t pop up every time. Untick it later if you want it back.
Holds a partial wake lock for the duration of the connection. Pings + GPS fire on time at the cost of higher battery drain.
When off (default): direction is inferred from motion when moving (speed > 2 km/h), last known motion-direction when stationary, compass as a final fallback. When on: always use the compass.
Ed25519 verification + auto-refresh of the server\'s long-term key.
Power-user knobs: ping cadence, viewer debug overlay, HTTP-server allow-list.
When off, an http:// viewer URL is blocked at connect time. Turning this on means a network MITM can forge /identity - verification becomes cosmetic.

Accepts any TLS cert on the /identity fetch - chain validation and hostname checks bypassed. For self-signed LAN servers. Same MITM risk as allowing HTTP: an attacker with a fake cert becomes indistinguishable from the real server.
Makes the on-screen state overlay visible in the browser. Useful in the field when devtools isn\'t reachable.
Tap to read the full policy.
This is a hobby / self-hostable project.\n\nThis application does not use accounts, analytics, ads, tracking, profiling, newsletters, or cookies.

Location sharing is end-to-end encrypted.

The server only relays encrypted data between clients. The server never receives the encryption key and cannot decrypt or inspect plaintext location data.

Location data is not saved to a database or persistent storage. Connection metadata may exist briefly in memory while the service is running.

The server, reverse proxy, hosting provider, Apache2, or similar infrastructure may create access logs.

These logs may include IP address, request time, requested URL, HTTP status code, user agent, referrer, and similar technical request data.

Log retention depends on server configuration. Logs may be rotated, deleted, or stored indefinitely. If no deletion or rotation is configured, logs may remain until manually removed.

The application may also print runtime logs to the terminal, stdout, stderr, systemd journal, Docker logs, screen/tmux, or another process manager.

By default, info-level logging is used. Debug logging may be enabled by configuration.

Runtime debug logs may include timestamps, ephemeral connection IDs, ephemeral share IDs or keys, internal status messages, viewer counts, errors, and connection/disconnection events.

Runtime logs do not contain plaintext location data. Logged share IDs, keys, or URLs may allow access to an encrypted live stream, but decrypting that stream requires the client-side encryption key, which is not sent to the server.
Data is processed only to provide the service, relay encrypted live updates, debug problems, prevent abuse, and keep the service secure.

Data collection is minimized as much as technically practical.

Data is not sold, rented, or shared for marketing.

Data may be processed by the server operator, hosting provider, reverse proxy, logging system, or other infrastructure used to run this instance.

This software can be self-hosted. Each instance is controlled by its own operator.\n\nThe operator of each instance is responsible for its own logging, retention, security, hosting, and legal compliance.
operator has not published a contact address

"THE BEERWARE LICENSE" (Revision 42):

DubbaThony wrote this code. As long as you retain this notice, you can do whatever you want with this stuff. If we meet someday, and you think this stuff is worth it, you can buy me a beer in return.

🍻
This project stands on the shoulders of these excellent open-source projects:

• AndroidX Core / Activity / DataStore
• Jetpack Compose (UI · Foundation · Material3)
• Kotlinx Coroutines
• BouncyCastle (Ed25519)
Compare what this APK is against what the server publishes. Useful when self-hosting to confirm the install on this phone matches the build you packaged into the server.
This APK matches the one offered by this server.
From the same publisher, but a different build than the server is offering.
This APK was signed by a different publisher than the server\'s.
The server didn\'t publish a build fingerprint - nothing to compare against.
Verifies the UDP handshake signature against a trusted Ed25519 key fetched over HTTPS. Turning this off makes an active MITM attacker able to read everything except the inner-encrypted GPS data.

On first connect to a new server, automatically fetch /identity over HTTPS. Off = the first connection refuses until you tap Refresh below. Never overwrites an existing identity on signature mismatch - that path goes through the explicit Refresh button.
How to surface a server-identity refresh from the background.
(no identity stored - first connect will trigger fetch)
Wipes the trusted Ed25519 key for %1$s. Next connect will need to re-fetch via /identity (or refuse if Auto-refresh is off).
Signal lost - payloads paused until lock returns.
No response from server for %1$d s - session still alive, protocol is retrying.
Seconds without a pong before this alert fires. The session is NOT killed - the spec\'s 600 s ctrl-deadline is the only kill path.
Master switch. When off, this alert never fires.
Heads-up system notification. Off = vibration only (if a pattern is picked).
Clears the system notification when the triggering condition goes away (lock returns, pong returns, new session starts, user dismisses dialog, ...).
%1$s:%2$d - UDP packet sent, no reply within %3$d s. Check that the server is running and that the host/port are correct in Settings.
Got frame type %1$d where HandshakeServer was expected. Possibly talking to the wrong service on %2$s:%3$d.
Got a HandshakeServer frame but couldn\'t parse it. Likely a protocol-version mismatch or the wrong server on this port.
App is newer than the server

The server at %1$s runs an older protocol than this app speaks. The matching older APK is most likely hosted on that same server - the Viewer URL tells you where to look.\n\nHeads up: installing the older APK will probably require uninstalling this one first. Different builds (and different self-hosters) sign with different keys, and Android refuses cross-key replacements.
App is older than the server

The server at %1$s expects a newer protocol than this app speaks. The newer APK that matches is hosted on that same server.\n\nHeads up: if the newer APK is signed differently from this install (different self-hoster, or rebuilt with a fresh key), Android will refuse to upgrade over the existing app - uninstall first.
Status code %1$d. No graceful recovery - try again, then check the server log.

The UDP handshake signature didn\'t match the trusted Ed25519 key for %1$s. Auto-refresh either failed or is disabled, so we can\'t tell whether this is a legitimate key rotation or a man-in-the-middle. Open Settings → Server identity, tap Refresh, and verify the new fingerprint out-of-band before reconnecting.
Server identity check failed (UDP MITM detected)

HTTPS confirmed the server\'s identity is unchanged, but the UDP handshake signature doesn\'t verify against it. Something is actively forging UDP traffic between this device and %1$s. The HTTPS channel appears intact - do not reconnect from this network.

Re-fetched the identity over HTTPS and got a DIFFERENT key, but the UDP handshake doesn\'t verify against it either. Either the server is misconfigured, or both the HTTPS and UDP channels are being intercepted. Do not reconnect.
Tried to connect with port %1$d - not a valid network port. Open Settings, set Server port to a value between 1 and 65535.
Server host is empty. Open Settings and set the server hostname or IP.
host=%1$s, port=%2$d, pingMs=%3$d\n\nOpen Settings, make sure host is a hostname/IP and port is 1-65535.
No trusted Ed25519 key cached for %1$s, and Auto-refresh is off. Open Settings → Server identity and tap Refresh, then reconnect.
No Viewer URL prefix configured to fetch /identity from. Open Settings, set the Viewer URL prefix (e.g. http://%1$s:8049/), and try again.
Tried to GET %1$s but: %2$s\n\nServer identity verification is required before any UDP traffic.
URL prefix not configured in Settings
Got past the first handshake step, then the server went quiet. Likely lost the UDP path or the server crashed after the first reply.
Got frame type %1$d where EncryptedHandshakeServer was expected.
Couldn\'t decrypt the server\'s reply - ECDH derived a different key on each side. Usually means a protocol version mismatch.
The server didn\'t acknowledge a control frame within %1$d s. Per protocol the connection is deemed dead. Toggle Server off then on to mint a fresh session.
ICMP port-unreachable from %1$s:%2$d. Either the server isn\'t listening, or it restarted and lost our session. Toggle Server off then on to make a fresh one.
%1$s: %2$s\n\nMost often: bad hostname (DNS), no network connectivity, or a firewall blocking UDP %3$d. Check Settings → Server host / port.
Location was turned off in system settings.
The server gracefully ended this session.
The server dropped the connection with a fatal error (no message provided). Toggle Server off then on to retry.
The server announced a clean shutdown. Try again when it\'s back.
